Clothing Manufacturing MOQ Calculator

Estimate your production costs at different quantities. These are real price ranges from our Sialkot factory — updated July 2026.

Cost Breakdown by Quantity

Quantity Cost Per Unit Decoration Labels Shipping Total Order Cost
Best Value:
These are estimated price ranges based on our current production costs at FABRIOZA. Final pricing depends on your specific design, fabric choice, and construction complexity. Request a precise quote for your project.

How to Use This Calculator

  1. Select your garment type and desired fabric weight
  2. Choose your decoration method (print, embroidery, or none)
  3. Select private labeling options
  4. Choose your shipping destination and method
  5. Click "Calculate" to see costs at 50, 100, 250, 500, and 1000 pieces

Understanding MOQ Pricing

Manufacturing cost per unit decreases as quantity increases because setup costs are spread across more pieces. Setup includes pattern making, machine calibration, and sample development. At FABRIOZA, our low MOQ of 50 pieces means you can test your designs with minimal investment.

What's Included in the Price

  • Pattern making and grading
  • Fabric sourcing and cutting
  • Sewing and construction
  • Quality control (4-stage inspection)
  • Standard poly bag packaging
  • Export documentation

What's NOT Included

  • Sample cost ($50-80, credited to bulk order)
  • Import duties/taxes (varies by country)
  • Custom packaging beyond standard poly bag

Get Your Exact Quote in 24 Hours

This calculator gives estimates. For precise pricing on your specific design, send us your tech pack or sketch.
